settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure RimWorld executable and mod directories.
settings.game_directory_desc: Folder containing Data/, Mods/, and game executable
settings.rimworld_exe_desc: Used for launching the game directly from the manager
settings.performance_level_desc: Choose how aggressive the app should be on older PCs and huge mod libraries.
settings.performance_normal_desc: Full visuals and the standard browsing experience.
settings.performance_mode_desc: Reduce thumbnail loading and UI effects for very large mod lists or older PCs.
settings.performance_mode_hint: Recommended for 1000+ mods or low-end hardware.
settings.ultra_performance_desc: Maximum stability for very weak hardware and extremely large libraries.
settings.performance_toggles_desc: Extra controls for huge libraries and older machines.
settings.disable_thumbnails_desc: Skip loading mod preview images in the list to save memory and disk reads.
settings.auto_update_mods_desc: Check for updates on startup and install newer workshop versions automatically.
settings.auto_suggest_performance_desc: Show a recommendation banner when the app detects a very large mod library.
settings.performance_suggestion_body: This library has {{count}} mods. Turning on Performance Mode can reduce lag on older PCs.
settings.texconv_desc: texconv.exe powers PNG→DDS optimization and smart resize. Downloaded from Microsoft DirectXTex.
settings.check_updates_desc: Scan all workshop mods to see which have newer versions (does not install).
settings.check_updates_none: All mods are up to date.
settings.backups_desc: Snapshots taken automatically before every load-order change. Restore to roll back.
settings.backups_restore_confirm: Restore this backup? Current load order will be overwritten (a new snapshot will be taken first).

guide.hero_subtitle: Become a RimWorld modding master. Detailed guide from basic setup to advanced optimization.
guide.setup_content:
• Game Directory: The folder containing RimWorldWin64.exe. The app needs this to identify Core mods and DLCs.
• AppData Directory: Where the game stores its mod configuration (ModsConfig.xml). This is the 'heart' of mod management; the app overwrites this to change your active mods.
• Tip: If the app can't find the game, double-check your path permissions in Settings.
guide.mods_content:
• Quick Toggle: Click (+) in the 'Inactive' list to enable, or (x) in the 'Active' list to disable.
• Smart Search: Find mods by name or author instantly.
• Custom Tags: Right-click any mod to add tags (e.g., 'Graphics', 'Gameplay'). You can then filter your list by these tags.
• Size Analysis: Use 'Analyze Sizes' to identify disk space hogs (especially high-res texture mods).
guide.order_content:
• Golden Rule: Core library mods (Core, HugsLib, JecsTools) must always be at the top.
• Auto-Sort: Powered by the RimPy community database. Click 'Auto-Sort' to automatically move mods to their safest positions.
• Conflict Analysis: Red-highlighted mods are missing dependencies or have placement issues. Read the 'Requires' section for fixes.
guide.opti_content:
• Why Optimize?: RimWorld mods often use PNGs, which your GPU must decompress. Converting to DDS (BC7) allows direct GPU reading, cutting load times from minutes to seconds.
• Smart Resize: For GPUs with <6GB VRAM, use 1024px. For high-end cards, keep 2048px.
• Note: This replaces original images in your mod folders. Restore via Steam's 'Verify Integrity' if needed.
guide.presets_content:
• Save Presets: Once you have a stable list, save it as a 'Collection'. Perfect for switching between different playthrough styles (e.g., Medieval vs. Cyberpunk).
• Automatic Sync: When a Profile is active, any changes you make in the Mods tab can be synced directly to that profile when you hit SAVE.
guide.trouble_content:
• Game Logs: If your game crashes, check the Logs tab. RED lines are your most important clues.
• System Restore: This re-downloads essential core files if they become corrupted or accidentally deleted.
• Clean Cache: Clearing the game's cache can often resolve strange texture glitches.
guide.community_desc: RimPro is built upon the contributions of the global RimWorld community. Check out these resources to further enhance your experience.

workshop_browser.title: Browse Steam Workshop
workshop_browser.subtitle: Browse Workshop inside the app and install mods with one click.
workshop_browser.not_a_mod_page: Open a mod detail page first.

crash.title: Crash Analyzer
crash.subtitle: Parse Player.log, surface culprit mods, optionally call AI for a fix plan.
crash.empty_hint: Click Analyze to scan Player.log. We auto-detect the default location across Win/Linux/Mac.
crash.ai_hint: Sends excerpt + heuristic suspects to your configured provider (Gemini BYOK or local Ollama). Configure in Settings.
